title: jwt.sign with algorithm: "none" — tokens are issued unsigned and can be forged by anyone

params.passDetail: jwt.sign issues the token with a real signing algorithm (not "none") — the token carries a verifiable signature.
params.failDetail: jwt.sign is called with algorithm: "none", so the issued token has an EMPTY signature. Anyone can craft a token with alg: none and arbitrary claims and it will pass verification wherever "none" is accepted — a total authentication bypass. Sign with a real algorithm (e.g. "HS256" or "RS256") and never issue unsigned tokens.
params.absentCallDetail: No jwt.sign call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: jwt.sign does not pass an algorithm literal; the library default (HS256) applies. Verify no code path forces "none".

provenance.note: The sign-side twin of the alg:none verify bypass: issuing tokens with algorithm "none" produces unsigned JWTs. Copied from snippets that 'disable signing to debug', it ships an auth system anyone can forge tokens against.
